message:
fix(widgets): extend NoStyling degradation handling to debug_overlay, pretty, and voi_debug_overlay

Direct continuation of the earlier "fix(widgets): respect NoStyling
degradation in help, history_panel, and constraint overlay" commit
(d8a889d1).  Three more widgets had the same latent bug: they
rendered unconditionally under every degradation level and baked
their configured Style values into cells without first checking
whether the frame's DegradationLevel actually permits styling or
content rendering.

The fix follows the same pattern established in d8a889d1: snapshot
`deg = frame.buffer.degradation` at the top, short-circuit when
`!deg.render_content()`, and substitute Style::default() / default
Cell contents when `!deg.apply_styling()`.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/debug_overlay.rs

- Widget::render(): check area.is_empty() first (defensive early
  return, same pattern as the other widgets) before the state
  enabled check.
- After the enabled check, add `let deg = frame.buffer.degradation;
  if !deg.render_content() { return; }` so Skeleton degradation
  short-circuits the entire overlay — previously Skeleton mode
  would still walk entries and draw borders/labels/hit rects.
- Border cell construction: wrap
    Cell::from_char('+').with_fg(color)
  in `if deg.apply_styling() { ... } else { Cell::from_char('+') }`
  so NoStyling renders a plain ASCII-only border without color.
- Label cell construction: same wrapping around the
    Cell::from_char(' ').with_fg(label_fg).with_bg(label_bg)
  builder — label appears as uncolored whitespace under NoStyling
  instead of the palette colors.
- Hit-region cell construction: same pattern for the
    Cell::from_char('.').with_fg(color)
  hit marker.
- New test render_short_truncation_handles_skeleton (placed
  alongside the existing short_render test) exercises Skeleton
  degradation against a populated overlay and asserts the buffer
  is untouched beyond the pre-existing sentinel cells.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/pretty.rs

- Widget::render(): snapshot deg, short-circuit on
  !deg.render_content(), and bind
    let style = if deg.apply_styling() { self.style } else { Style::default() };
  before the formatted-text draw loop.  The per-line
  draw_text_span call then reads from the local `style` variable
  instead of self.style directly.
- New tests:
    render_no_styling_drops_configured_style — builds a Pretty
      widget with a fg(red)+bold style, renders under NoStyling,
      and asserts the first buffer cell has the default fg/bg/
      attrs and not the configured red+bold.
    render_skeleton_is_noop — builds a default Pretty widget,
      renders under Skeleton, and asserts the target buffer cell
      still holds Cell::default() (no content written at all).

crates/ftui-widgets/src/voi_debug_overlay.rs

- Widget::render(): insert the deg snapshot + render_content
  short-circuit immediately after the existing area-empty guard.
- Background-fill path: wrap the existing `if let Some(bg) =
  self.style.background` in a paired check
    `if deg.apply_styling() && let Some(bg) = self.style.background`
  so NoStyling skips the full-area fill cell entirely (the
  default Cell::default().with_bg(bg) would have rewritten every
  cell in the overlay area with a solid color even under
  NoStyling).
- New tests:
    render_no_styling_drops_background_fill — builds a
      VoiDebugOverlay with a non-default background color,
      renders under NoStyling into an 80x32 frame, and asserts
      cell (2,2) is back to the default Cell bg instead of the
      configured one.
    render_skeleton_is_noop — pre-populates two sentinel cells
      in the buffer, renders the overlay under Skeleton, and
      asserts both sentinels survived untouched.

Together with d8a889d1 every Widget that binds colors/backgrounds
into Cells now correctly degrades to plain content under NoStyling
and short-circuits to no-op under Skeleton.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
